# Anchor Suite - agent and LLM reference

Base URL: https://anchor.ainumbers.co

## Pipeline position

Anchor Suite is step 4 of 5 in the AINumbers.co pipeline:
  ① Learn   - https://ainumbers.co (docs, education hub, explainers)
  ② Run     - https://ainumbers.co + https://mcp.ainumbers.co (tools, workflows, agents)
  ③ Verify  - https://ledger.ainumbers.co (receipts land and are checked here)
  ④ Anchor  - https://anchor.ainumbers.co (independent timestamp and signature) -- YOU ARE HERE
  ⑤ Keep   - https://ledger.ainumbers.co (export and share from the Ledger)

After an agent runs a workflow on ainumbers.co and receipts appear at ledger.ainumbers.co,
use Anchor Suite (step 4) to timestamp the execution_hash with independent authorities.
The resulting anchor_bindings attach to the artifact outside the hash scope per OCG v0.7 section 20.
Verified receipts return to the Ledger (step 5) for export and sharing.

## What this site does

Anchor Suite lets you prove that a file or data artifact existed at a specific point in time.
Hash computation runs in the browser (WebCrypto SHA-256). Only the 32-byte hash is sent to
timestamp authorities. No file content leaves the browser.

## Pages

- / - Overview and how-it-works
- /anchor.html - Stamp tool. Accepts file drop, paste of hex digest, or #eh=sha256:<hex> fragment deep-link.
- /verify.html - Verifier. Accepts anchors.json, OCG artifact JSON, raw DER TST, or .ots file.
- /docs/ - Full documentation: binding format, openssl recipe, TSA facts, pinned roots.

## anchor.html deep-link

Fragment: #eh=sha256:<64-hex-chars>
Example: https://anchor.ainumbers.co/anchor.html#eh=sha256:abc123...

This pre-fills the hash field. The fragment is never sent to the server.

## anchors.json format

POST result - a file with one top-level key:

  { "anchor_bindings": [ <binding>, ... ] }

Each binding is one of:

rfc3161-tst binding:
  {
    "type": "rfc3161-tst",
    "anchored_hash": "sha256:<64 hex>",
    "log_origin": "<TSA URL>",
    "proof": "<base64 DER TimeStampToken>",
    "policy_oid": "<OID>",
    "serial": "<hex>",
    "gen_time": "<ISO 8601>",
    "signer_cert_chain_b64": ["<base64 DER>", ...]
  }

opentimestamps binding:
  {
    "type": "opentimestamps",
    "anchored_hash": "sha256:<64 hex>",
    "log_origin": "bitcoin",
    "proof": "<base64 .ots bytes>"
  }

## Relay endpoints (for RFC 3161 CAs that block browser CORS)

POST /relay/digicert   - pipes to http://timestamp.digicert.com
POST /relay/sectigo    - pipes to http://timestamp.sectigo.com (15s pacing enforced)
POST /relay/freetsa    - pipes to https://freetsa.org/tsr

Content-Type: application/timestamp-query  (DER TimeStampReq, max 2 KB)
Response:     application/timestamp-reply  (DER TimeStampResp verbatim)
CORS:         allows https://anchor.ainumbers.co only
Rate limit:   4 requests per minute per IP

## Direct TSA endpoints (CORS-open, no relay needed)

Sigstore TSA:
  POST https://timestamp.sigstore.dev/api/v1/timestamp
  Body: { "artifactHash": "<base64>", "hashAlgorithm": "sha256", "nonce": <number>, "certificates": true }
  Response: DER TimeStampToken

GitHub TSA:
  POST https://timestamp.githubapp.com/api/v1/timestamp
  Body: DER TimeStampReq (application/timestamp-query)
  Response: DER TimeStampResp
  Note: undocumented for third-party use

## Verification (independent of this site)

openssl ts -verify -digest <sha256-hex> -in tst.der -CAfile <root.pem>

Root PEM files:
  public/vendor/roots/digicert-trusted-root-g4.pem
  public/vendor/roots/sectigo-time-stamping-root-r46.pem
  public/vendor/roots/freetsa-cacert.pem
  public/vendor/roots/sigstore-tsa-root.pem
  public/vendor/roots/github-tsa-root.pem

## OpenChainGraph support

Drop an OCG artifact (JSON with execution_hash, policy_parameters, output_payload) on /anchor.html.
The tool anchors execution_hash (not the raw file hash) per OCG v0.7 section 20.
Download produces the artifact re-emitted with anchor_bindings[] appended at the top level,
outside the execution_hash scope.

Drop an OCG artifact on /verify.html to run:
  Section 4  - execution_hash recompute
  Section 16 - eddsa-jcs-2022 Data Integrity signature
  Section 18 - Groth16-BN254 compute proof seal
All client-side, offline-capable after first page load.

## Artifact Library (/artifacts.html)

Locally stores verified OCG artifacts and anchors.json receipts in IndexedDB (no server).
Import: drop zone, file picker, paste JSON from clipboard.
Features: pin, reorder (move up/down), filter by hash or tag, detail view with badge row, re-verify, export, copy hash.
"Anchor this" button deep-links to /anchor.html#eh=sha256:<hash> with hash pre-filled.
Storage durability: navigator.storage.persist() is requested; iOS Safari may evict without it.
Library data never leaves the device.

## MCP

Endpoint: https://anchor.ainumbers.co/mcp
Protocol: MCP JSON-RPC (streamable HTTP, POST, Content-Type: application/json)
CORS: open (any origin; agents are not browsers)

### Tools

list_anchor_authorities()
  Returns the six supported timestamp authorities with live health status.
  Each entry: { id, name, kind: "rfc3161"|"opentimestamps", cost, notes, healthy: bool|null }

anchor_hash({ hash, authorities })
  hash: SHA-256 digest as 64 hex characters, optionally prefixed with sha256:
  authorities: array of one or more authority ids (sigstore, digicert, sectigo, freetsa, github, opentimestamps)
  Returns: { anchor_bindings: [...], failures: [{ authority, reason }] }
  Each binding is an OCG v0.7 section 20 rfc3161-tst or opentimestamps binding.
  The Worker calls authorities directly with no CORS constraint.
  Rate-limited: 4 requests per minute per IP. Sectigo paced automatically.
  Nothing is stored.

verify_anchor_binding({ binding } | { anchors } | { artifact })
  Accepts a single binding, an anchors.json object, or a full OCG artifact.
  Returns: { results: [{ type, valid, tsa, gen_time, policy_oid, serial, reasons }] }
  Uses the same verification logic as the browser verify page (verify parity).
  OpenTimestamps bindings return pending status until Bitcoin confirms.

### Agent flow

1. Compute OCG execution_hash (or any SHA-256 digest).
2. Call anchor_hash with 2+ authorities to get anchor_bindings[].
3. Attach anchor_bindings[] to the artifact outside the execution_hash scope (OCG v0.7 section 20).
4. Later, call verify_anchor_binding to confirm each binding chains to a pinned root.

## Security

CSP: script-src 'self'; connect-src exact allowlist; require-trusted-types-for 'script'
No analytics, no third-party scripts, no CDNs.
Source: https://github.com/PostOakLabs/anchor-suite
